context.scenario:
I (15) have pretty much been vegetarian for ethical/moral reasons since I was 10, in the last five years I've been vegan or an omnivore too for a short while but I have not consumed animal meat in a long time.

EDIT: I make all of my meals but I don't turn down anything she makes for me.

My mum recently was hospitalised (an ambulance was called and stuff but she's okay now) and I had nothing to do while I stayed with her in hospital so she let me borrow her phone (mine doesn't have much data whilst she has like unlimited) to search up vegetarian mukbangs because I was really hungry and there wasn't food around. Whilst doing that, something accidentally caught my eye (I was searching stuff up on an app that had both short form content -- like yt shorts and also messages to friends) and I saw that she had sent a message to a friend of hers saying that 'It's so hard to cook for my kid now. I just want to sneak some beef or lamb into her food, add some tofu or something so she can't tell' and I immediately felt every emotion you can name basically.

I didn't intentionally go through her messages or anything, I do feel bad for accidentally seeing but since it's about me I think I had the right to be upset.

I didn't tell her immediately but I knew that when I was 10-11 and just starting vegetarianism for ethical reasons she had snuck fish meat into my food quite a few times (I knew because I overheard her bragging? to it to her friends wasn't apologetic at all?) and this just made me so frustrated and upset.

I knew my mum wasn't well so I waited a few days until the day before she was being discharged to talk to her about it.

I sort of just said 'hey mum, I wanted to talk to you about something. I accidentally saw these messages on your phone and I was wondering why you'd do this...'

And then she got pretty defensive, telling me she had never ever done that ever before. And then I told her that I could literally get up the messages on her phone which was tangible proof, and that I could understand that she may have had her reasons (like being scared I'd get deficiencies??) but all I wanted was an apology.

My grandpa, who was watching from the sidelines ended up yelling at me ('your mother just got discharged from hospital and this is what you say?? You're really a considerate kid (sarcastic)') even though I was the one primarily looking after her/bringing her gifts/talking to her during her hospital stay..

And my mum just looked at me and said there was nothing she should be apologetic about. I asked her again and she just repeated that.

And I remember staring at her (tears welling up in my eyes haha) and telling her that I was going to walk home from the hospital now and that I hope her discharging goes well tomorrow.

Did I do anything wrong? I really don't understand, all I wanted was an apology.

Thank you for reading :)

edit: more info:

she said she shouldn't be sorry about it because she only had the thoughts and never carried them out RECENTLY (she did when I was younger) but I think it may have just been because the friend she was talking to told her that I'd be able to tell and that she shouldn't do it.

She gave me sauce 3 times like a month ago that contained fish and other seafoods and when I realised she claimed it was simply because she didn't read the label.

Am I right to be upset????
context.comment:
YTA. You are still a child and your mother is responsible for your care and wellbeing. So that means she gets the final say in what you can do until you're old enough to do so for yourself. Which means that if she really wanted to, she could stop you from being a vegetarian and make you eat what she says. Something that you wouldn't be able to overturn since you are a minor and will be so for many years to come.

Yet she hasn't done any of that, despite her worries for you. She has allowed you to take on a vegetarian lifestyle. That's not something every parent will do. So instead of getting on your high horse and thinking that she owes you an apology, you need to think about what you owe her. Because she doesn't have to let you do anything but does just the same.

context.scenario:
I (23f) recently started a new job as server at a restaurant this week. It's been okay, I honestly don't like how everyone is overly friendly at work but a job is a job right?

This afternoon, I finally meet this co-worker (32m) who works from Wednesdays to Sundays. I shook his hands when he came in to introduce myself. He raised my hand up, looked at me up and down, and spun me around 360 to check out my body.

I was just in shock, I didn't know what was happening. He then told me I had a nice figure and he wishes he had a butt like mine.

At that point, I was all red. I was just like what?? and he immediately apologized, saying he was just complimenting my body and then asked for my gym routine. 

Other co-workers were around us and they saw I was clearly uncomfortable so they jumped in and just told me he is just like that. In the background, I heard him trying to tell me he is gay and he didn't mean anything bad by the comment.

But at that point, my head was spinning and I was infuriated. Sure I had men catcalled me and said inappropriate things but not like this, not in workplace by a co-worker, not with other co-workers watching. It was a really embarrassing situation.

I clocked out about half an hour after that. I worked day shift and he usually does night shift, so luckily we didn't have to work together today. He just got here a little early and spent the rest of the time in the break room talking with others. He seems super loud and way too friendly with others as well, he doesn't watch what he says.

I immediately texted my manager, who didn't come in because it was his only day off. I then emailed the HR department including all details and asked for my co-worker to be fired because what he did and said was simply too inappropriate. He seems to be a popular guy at work and brings in a lot of tips so I don't know if they will really fire him or just give a warning.

AITA for asking him to be fired? Am I going too far?
context.comment:
YTA. Your attitude shows just how young and entitled you are. It's not up to you to decide who gets fired or not. That is for your manager, HR and the company to decide. And I can tell you now that no one will fire him over just one incident. Especially if there are not prior records of him treating anyone like that. If anything, they will speak with him, maybe enforce a write-up and coach him on more appropriate behavior.

When it comes to you however, all you've done is show your coworkers is that you're a whiny tattletale who goes crying to the higher ups at the slightest provocation. They aren't going to see you as a fellow adult. All they are going to see is someone too immature to deal with. Thus they will avoid you as much as possible so that none of them have to deal with your nonsense. Which is why I suggest you get your head out of your ass and start thinking before you act or make demands of anyone.

context.scenario: I (F16) have been dating my boyfriend (M17) for about 7 months now and this month we will be 8 months. I am very awkward (keep this in mind) but I always try to find time to hangout with my boyfriend when I can but luck just isn’t my thing so when he does want to I am either busy or mentally drained that day. I am extremely happy in my relationship and I don’t want anyone else but lately we have not been hanging out much and he’s expressed that and we communicated and I told him I know how he feels and how I’ll do better but today is his bday dinner and I was supposed to go but my grandpa was found to be sick that same day. I WAS TERRIFIED to tell him and not because his reaction would be bad but because I’d feel so guilty afterwards and I already told him I am introverted and hate eating infront of people so this just makes it seem even more like an excuse, I told him I couldn’t go because I had to take care of my grandpa and he seemed pretty mad but I can also see why he is because over time it has seemed like I’m avoiding him but I’m not and I’ve expressed that. I don’t know what to do because he isn’t very good at communication. Maybe I could’ve left and came back to my grandpa but he’s old and what if I came back to him already at the pearly white gates. I was looking forward to this dinner but family does come first..AITA??
context.comment:
YTA. Because right now, you're only thinking of me, me, me. Not once have you stopped and considered your boyfriend's feelings. Who, if I'm gonna be honest, it probably getting pretty tired of being pushed to the sidelines just because you're feeling worn out or uncomfortable with a situation.

Which, if it were a once in awhile thing, wouldn't be a big deal. You take some time to yourself. But this seems to be a regular occurrence for you. And with it happening so often, you really are being selfish. As part of a relationship is knowing that there are times when you need to put your partner first, even if it means doing things that you don't like. A lesson that you obviously have yet to learn.

context.scenario:
My (26F) sister (24) has been with her partner (34M) for around 5 years, and they have a soon-to-be 4yo together.

 I invited my sister to celebrate my birthday, and she asked if he could also come. I only feel like TA because my boyfriend was welcome to celebrate her birthday with us (even though he didn't come.)

The problem is, dude drives me up the wall. He's very immature for his age, like he got stuck at 16. He isn't the brightest, and likes to try to butt into conversations and talk like he understands the subject when he obviously has no clue. He's a "sovereign citizen," really into conspiracy theories, and likes to wear a belt with a hunting knife on it everywhere he goes. He isn't rude, per se, he just doesn't know how to socialize appropriately for his age, and sometimes it feels like my sister is babysitting him. She literally has to tell him what to do or not to do half the time.

I didn't want to invite him when I hosted Christmas this last year, but felt like I had to because it wouldn't be fair to my niece to not have both of her parents around. 

So, AITA??
context.comment:
YTA. Sorry but we all have relatives and in-laws that we don't like. It's just part of being family. For instance, I can't stand my husband's aunt. She is loud, obnoxious and never knows when to shut up. I'd rather kiss the business end of a porcupine than deal with her. But I suck it up and deal with her because she is my husband's aunt and therefore there are just times when I have to deal with her for the sake of family harmony.

In reverse, my husband can't stand being around most of my family. Save for my great aunt and a few of my younger cousins, he thinks that most of my family is a bunch of snobs. But just like I put up with his aunt, he puts up with them for my sake.

context.scenario:
My daughter "Nina" (22F) got pregnant young with my grandson "Tate" (8M) by her ex "Derek" (23M). As soon as he found out she was pregnant he suddenly realized he was gay and broke up with her for some boy. After that neither I or my daughter wanted him around Tate after he was born. The kid made several attempts to try to see Tate but we shut him out and we got full custody in court as his parents didn't have enough money to fight it.

He finally left us alone and went off to college. My daughter goes to the local college and when she has classes and me and my wife are working my sister and my niece "Ashley" (21F) watch Tate. Friday  me and my wife got off work early and went to pick up Tate.  We pull up  to my sister's and I see this this random boy smoking leaning against an unknown car. I thought he might be my niece's boyfriend and I walked over and asked and he told me no that he was with Derek. I was beyond pissed and I stormed in the house and saw Derek talking to Tate and I yelled at him to get out. He told Tate bye and scrammed out of there and i sent Tate outside with my wife. I was furious and I asked how long has this been going on.

My sister told me to calm down but I wasn't haven't it I asked again how long and she told me at least  the last few years now. That Derek would come home and asked her and Ashley to see him since him and Ashley used to be friends. I was screaming now and I was why would you even let him around after he'd abandoned her niece  my daughter. Ashley starts yelling back at me that he can't help being but we were the ones that made him abandon Tate.I tell her that he should've thought of that before impregnating my daughter. My sister chimes in saying all he did was spend time with his kid  they hadn't even told Tate he was his dad just that he was Ashley's friend "D" . I tell her never again and I leave.  I talked it over with my wife and she thinks we may have been to hard on Derek and that I probably shouldn't have yelled at them like that. Aita?
context.comment:
YTA. He made an effort to be in his kid's life. Most guys in these situations don't do that. They just turn tail and NEVER look back. Because they're too chicken to deal with the consequences of their actions. At least Derek tried to step up. Only to be shut down by a bunch of self-righteous idiots. 

And you mark my words. This shitty attitude is gonna come back to bite you all. Because Tate will not remain a child forever. He's gonna grow up and start asking questions about where his father is. Then he's gonna learn the truth of how things went down. When he does, the shit is gonna hit the fan and you're gonna be the bull's eye. Just hope you can deal with that.

context.scenario:
so basically, i (f17) got into an argument with my brother (m15), who i’m going to call noah, in the car the other day about this. 

almost a year ago, i fell absolutely head over heels for my ex boyfriend (m16) who we’ll call connor. connor’s younger brother, who we’ll call jason (m15). noah and jason are BEST friends. like. jason practically lives here in the summer when noah is allowed to have long sleepovers. they play hockey together. connor also plays hockey, and we met briefly a few times before but started going out in august of 2024. connor is also friends with noah. long story short, connor had a lot going on with sports (hockey and baseball) and school. we stopped hanging out as much and he eventually broke up with me (like a week before valentine’s day but i’m not going to be petty about that here) because he felt he wasn’t giving me the time and attention i deserved. i was so upset. we ended on good terms, but i’m not going to sit here and say i’m not hurt because i’m not even sure i’m actually over him yet. 

i live in the northwestern part of america. my dad got a job in the south so we’re moving there three weeks from now. my brothers are super upset about it and my mom has been pushing us all to spend as much time with our friends as we can. 

i was driving home from school with noah and he dropped it on me that he was going to have a sleepover with jason, conner, and another one of their friends. i was like, oh, that sounds fun, i feel bad for whoever’s parents are hosting that. 

and he told me that my mom said he could have it at our house. 

so immediately i was super upset, because i’m not going to give up my safe space and not be able to come out of my room without it being awkward with connor around, and i protested. i was like— “that’s going to be so awkward, i am not comfortable with that, can’t you have it somewhere else?” and he rolled his eyes. 

he told me that i needed to go find somewhere else to spend the weekend because he was going to have this sleepover no matter what and my mom told him he could only do it if i went to a friend’s house for the weekend or something. most people would think that’s a great deal and take it, but i HATE sleepovers. i have a lot of anxiety surrounding them and i just don’t like not being able to spend the night in my own house. i was also going to get some things done this weekend. i told him i absolutely would not be leaving and he would have to figure something out and he was PISSED. 

he called me selfish and said i should just grow up and stop being petty but i feel like im completely justified in keeping myself comfortable and not letting my brother kick me out of my own house so he can have my ex boyfriend over for a sleepover. am i the asshole? i’ve gotten some mixed reactions from my friends and maybe im overreacting but i don’t know. :(

i want to make an edit to clarify something. the four of them can have a sleepover at anyone else's house, my brother just wants it to be here because he doesn't want to pack. our house is pretty small and they're all very destructive together, and that's part of the reason i don't want to stay home and "mind my business". my room is right next to noah's and they stay up all night screaming and yelling and messing around. i am not preventing them from ever seeing each other again, i just don't want it to happen here. i feel very violated by my brother demanding i go somewhere else even though i don't really have anywhere else to go. we have a lot of family here, so we are planning on visiting often. my parents are very close with conner's parents and said that noah could go spend a couple weeks at their house this summer. this is not a forever goodbye that i am interrupting, it would just be the last sleepover before we leave.
context.comment:
YTA and a selfish, entitled brat. This is going to be one of the last times that your brother will see his friends. So they deserve a chance to spend time together. If that means you suck it up and go somewhere else for a night or two, then do it. Because I've got news for you, little girl.

You're almost an adult. And one of the things that you're gonna have to learn is that a lot of the time, you have to do things you don't like or want to do. You can't just whine and say you don't want to give up your safe space or some other nonsense like that. It doesn't work that way. In fact, there can often be serious consequences for such an attitude. Something you're gonna learn the hard way in the near future.

context.scenario:
Me (36f) and my husband Rob (45m) live together with my kids, Caleb (15m) and Angel (11f) full-time, their dad is flaky. Rob's daughter Kady (16f) comes off every weekend. We are also expecting a baby boy in November.

Kady is angry about our marriage and has made her displeasure known, she gives me and my kids the cold shoulder and only speaks to us when she is forced to. Rob and his ex were separated for about two years before divorcing, in no part to me. We knew each other socially through my brother but we didn't start anything until his divorce was finalized. We got married fast 7 months after because I was honestly gun-shy of wasting time without a commitment due to my kids’ father stringing me along for years. 

I have spent the past year trying to make nice with Kady but she continually freeze us out. My daughter is shy so she pretty much enjoy being on her own or hanging out with her friends. But Caleb doesn't like Kady and has called her a ‘bitch’ and ‘asshole’ behind her back which I corrected. 

This week, my son had friends over and I guess he told them not to acknowledge her because they spent the whole time not looking at her and pretending she wasn't there. When she came into the door, they would pretend the door opened by itself and fake freaked out like it was a ghost. They ordered food and didn't order anything for her, and didn't share what they ordered. There was regular food in the house and she had a debit card but she got pissed and called her dad, telling him what was going on. Caleb started laughing at her and called her a snitch and that turned into a shouting match. At this point, I was coming home and got the story from Caleb and his friends. I sent the friends home and sent them both to their rooms to cool down. I felt things were settled, siblings fight, but it's not that deep. 

Rob came home heated and feels Caleb should apologize because he's “bullying” Kady. I told him if Caleb ignoring her was bullying then Kady is the biggest bully in the house. I told him if he wanted an apology from Caleb and his friends, Kady needed to apologize also to me and my kids. He keep insisting that they boys went too far but I'm over it and told him if Kady couldn't handle it she could stay home with her mom or he can spend weekends at a hotel with her alone.
AITA for not making my son apologize?
context.comment:
YTA. By not disciplining him, you're making it clear to your son that such actions will continue to be tolerated. As in, he can say or do whatever he wants to Kady and not worry about you punishing him. That is just going to make things escalate. Which is definitely not okay.

What makes it worse is that you seem to forget that Kady is going through a tougher time than your daughter and that brat you call a son. Because you're there all the time and so is your husband, their stepfather. Kady only gets to see him twice a month and on the weekends. During which she has to share his attention with a whole new family. Instead of being such a witch, try thinking about how that makes her feel for a change.

And honestly, if all Kady does is ignore you and rarely speaks to you, that isn't being a bully. That's just someone keeping their distance. Whereas your son and his friends went out of their way to be nasty to Kady. Showing that they are little snots in need of some serious discipline. Something you need to step up and handle before her dad decides that you aren't worth his time. As she is his daughter and she will always come before you.

context.scenario:
My husband and I both have NPD, aka we're narcissists, so we can't always tell who's in the right. I've gotten amazing advice from Reddit before so I'm giving it another go! 

So basically, I was having a ROUGH day. Like, one of the shittiest days ever. I got ghosted after my audition for this big show, and I was thinking, things are probably just held up, you know? They'll get back to me. But then they posted the cast list ON SOCIAL MEDIA, and they already started REHEARSALS. And didn't even bother to let me know I didn't get in, even in the ensemble. It's not like it was a Broadway show or a national tour even, and I just felt so angry, like where do they get off not telling people? I cleared my schedule for this show and figured I'd at least be ensemble, and they don't even reach out? It's ridiculous. 

Anyway. I was just venting about it for a while, and I was complaining that it was because of my body. My body is larger than I want it to be ever since I had my daughter, but like, I brought a life into this world, and that's its own beauty. Anyway, I was complaining about the misogyny I face daily as a mother, and my husband, who is supposed to be on my side, said if I don't like my body I should just change it. Like, oh, sorry, did you push a baby out? Did you form the most perfect being in the world out of your own bone marrow? But oh, please, tell me how easy it is to go back to fitting into your wedding dress, I'd love to hear it. 

Anyway, we had karaoke tonight and I sang "Ugly Heart," by G.R.L. And he got all pissed at me and stormed out halfway through and didn't even bother to finish recording the song, he just left the phone set up to point at the stage and I couldn't even move without going out of frame. And he asked why I'd sing that and I said it's because it's true, and if he doesn't want to have an ugly heart he should "just change it."

He's currently sleeping in the guest room with our daughter and I heard him crying and saying she's the only one who'll always love him, and I feel kind of horrible. I know I'm probably being unreasonable. I know I'm probably taking the musical thing out on him a little, but I feel like he kind of deserves it after that jab about my body, and he's overreacting. 

AITA? 

  
TL;DR: Husband said if I don't like my body, change it. I said if he doesn't like his ugly heart, change it. AITA?
context.comment:
YTA. Because your husband is right. If you're not happy with your body, then stop complaining and do something about it. The only one who can make that happen is you after all. So stop whining and get your ass on the walk track. 

Second, you do realize that even though you gave birth, your husband is just as much a part of creating life as you are. For the simple fact that without him, there would be no life. As he is the one that sired the child. Yet you, along with so many others, seems to forget that fact. As you seem to think that fathers have no worth during the pregnancy. 

Third, the only one out of this entire mess with an ugly heart is you. Because you're taking all your frustrations out on your husband. Then you have the nerve to complain about him getting upset and thinking that you're the victim. When the truth is, you're not. So get over yourself and go apologize to him for being such a snot.

context.scenario:
I (50s) lost my job during COVID and haven’t been able to find work since. It’s been incredibly tough, and I’ve been surviving on what little support I get. My adult child (in their late 20s) gives me $600 a month to help with household expenses like food, water, and electricity. They live with me but pay for their own personal expenses like transport, food, and clothing.

I recently brought up that I’m struggling to keep up with the bills. I mentioned how they’ve been getting raises at work while my support from them has stayed the same, and that unfortunately, $600 just isn’t enough anymore. That led to a big argument.

They said it’s not fair that they’re basically surviving on half an income to keep the household going, and that I should find a job. I understand where they’re coming from, they’re young and trying to build their own life, but I’m also at my wits’ end. I feel stuck, frustrated, and overwhelmed. I didn’t say it to guilt them. I was just being honest about my situation.

I also want to add some more context I left out in my original post (which I shared in another group, but I’d appreciate more feedback here too)
I’ve been renting out a room in the house, but I’m not seeing any income from that yet because my child took out a loan to build the extra room, and the rent is currently going toward repaying that loan. I’ve also tried different hustles, selling food, jewelry, household products, but people often promise to pay me on payday and then ghost me. I end up losing money I already didn’t have.

On top of that, I don’t have xtra money for Uber or transport, and trying to do my license takes money I simply don’t have.

AITA for bringing this up to my child? Or just for expecting more support? I really wasn’t trying to be ungrateful, just trying to be honest about how hard things are right now
context.comment:
YTA. I was fired from my job back in May and spent the entire weekend filling out applications on Indeed. I wasn't picky about what I got. Just as long as I got something to support my family until I found something stable. Had more than a dozen interviews in one week and got hired as a manager at an eatery in less than two. I worked there for two months until I got the job that I have now and things are fine.

It's possible to get a job. You just have to be willing to take what you can and then go with that until something better comes. So stop pestering your kid and do something about it.

context.scenario:
I (21f) live with my grandmother. I have this cousin who is in her 30s who comes over occasionally to visit her as shes not in the best of health. She brings her kids and sometimes two younger boys she is babysitting for another family member. 

I was out for a while on this day with my partner and a close friend. On the way back to my house I get a text from my sister that one of the little boys she brought with her broke my 1987 LEGO Batmobile set as well as a zen garden set we had out in the living room as decoration. When I get home i see most of the back end of the set is on the floor and shelf my sister picked up the main part of the set and most of the pieces she could find and put it on the kitchen counter out of reach. Only one of the trees ended up being broken on the garden set.

 While im assesing the damage my partner has to move my food out of the way of the child because he was visibly going for it. I want to make it clear my partner was fairly respectful doing this only being firm and moving the bag. They then went back to our room and shut the door.

 We have a towel hook on out door that catches when its uneven and so it made a fairly loud noise when the door was shut. It was not slammed but my cousin starts screaming about how disrespectful that was and that we dont pay enough to be slamming doors in the house. She does not live her and consistently flakes when agreeing to help out grandma. She continues to holler about dirty the house is (Its not) how we don't do enough for grandma (Im her primary care giver) and that I shouldnt be upset that the kid broke my set because hes autistic.

 This is where i may be the asshole. I yelled back tha I don't care that hes autistic and she should have been watching him. She then storms out with all four kids in tow yelling basically nonsense. 

My partner is autistic and we both know not all autistic people are alike but she was basically equating his autism to an inability to learn or understand right from wrong. 

We have had other incidents with her disrespecting me and my partner for being part of the queer community and most of her issues seem to be with my partner having to move in with my family for their own safety.

In case you're wondering we still haven't managed to put the set back together.

I am mostly here to see if i should just suck it up and apologize to keep the peace.
context.comment:
NTA. I get so tired of people assuming that they can use the fact that a person is autistic as an excuse to just let someone get away with making a mess or causing trouble. No. Actions have consequences. That's just a simple fact of life.

Plus, if he's a little kid, then he should've been watched like a hawk regardless of his diagnosis. As little kids are magnets for trouble and general chaos. That too is a simple fact of life. But the fact that your cousin is still using this as an excuse just shows what a shitty parent she is to begin with.

context.scenario:
AITAH?

My brother passed away 2 months ago. His partner is still refusing to commit to a service or Celebration of Life. I messaged tonight asking again and begged for him to have it soon as his family and friends need to celebrate him and find closure, and he deserves to be remembered. His partner blasted me, saying "How dare you to pressure and rush things", among other things. Than messaged "I am furious" AITAH?

Edit... I have offered to organize but got blasted for that as well :(
context.comment: NTA. Who says that you need his partner's permission to do anything? You have your own memories and stories of your brother. Have something for yourself and others. If his partner doesn't like it, then boo on them. That guy doesn't have the right when to tell others how and when they can mourn.

scenario:
I (38 M) have been married to my wife (34 F) for 7 years. We have 4 kids together, aged 7,6,4,and 4. 6yo and 4yo are boys, 7yo and 4yo are girls. Me and my wife got into a pretty big argument recently and I need unbiased opinions. 

I feel like I've always been closer to my sons rather than my daughters. You know I'm a boy, I like boy stuff, so do they. I don't like princesses or dolls. So I've always gravitated towards my sons because we have more in common. 

Anyways the argument started after my wife had put the kids to bed one night. I was laying down and she came into the room and confronted me about what my 7yo daughter had just told her. According to my wife, as she was tucking my daughter into bed she started crying and asked, “ Why doesn't daddy like me”. My wife told me that the kids notice how I treat them differently and I needed to stop acting like I hated my daughters. I told her I don't hate them, but we don't have anything in common. She was pissed and started yelling at me about how immature I was being immature. I think it's stupid. Obviously dads are going to be closer to their sons, that's just how the brain works. I tried explaining this to her and she just didn't listen. She left and I think she went to sleep in my daughter's bed. I'm not sure.

So am I the asshole? I just want my wife to understand what I'm saying and she's not listening to me.
question: Based on this person's historical commenting patterns, which moral principle would they MOST likely use to judge this scenario?
answer_options: A. Care/Harm Prevention: The judgment is primarily justified by concern about direct physical or emotional suffering of a person (victim-centered empathy, trauma, distress).
B. Role-based Responsibility: The judgment is primarily justified by duties tied to a specific functional role (tenant/roommate/employee/co-parent) rather than emotion-based loyalty.
C. Autonomy/Boundaries: The judgment is primarily justified by violation of personal sovereignty: controlling behavior, unwanted interference, privacy invasion, or boundary crossing (non-material).
D. Property/Consent: The judgment is primarily justified by violations involving ownership, money, physical possessions, or bodily access without permission.
